title: Lovesac
tagline: Modular kit-of-parts buried in carousel slide 1. Variants make the system the page's visual language without rebranding it.

source.category: DTC commerce · modular furniture · lifetime promise

direction.phrase: Refresh the existing brand. Fix the chrome clutter and type-scale drift. Amplify the captured modular-system / Designed For Life story — make the kit-of-parts visible. 3 variants.
movements: expressive: restrained → committed across all variants; distinctive at B and C
density: balanced (default; brand-register multi-audience hard floor applies)
distinctiveness: familiar → distinctive for B (kit-bracket motif) and C (year-rule motif)
audience: preserved tuple per Mode A IA-priority preservation
constraints: brand-faithful (Mode A active), commercial-conversion preservation

originalCritique: A signal-strong brand surface buried under commerce chrome. The Spring Sale promo squats in the header on 3 of 5 captured pages; the type scale is ad-hoc (three different ratios: 1.222 / 1.125 / 1.143); 12 above-fold CTAs compete for attention with a fixed CHAT widget rendered as the page's primary button colour. The captured wordmark is icon-mark only (18×19 SVG), and the modular kit-of-parts that justifies the entire Sactionals product line is buried inside slide 1 of an 8-slide carousel. Palette is single-context — `#0098a7` brand teal appears almost exclusively as button fill. The strongest assets — the modular system, the Designed For Life® promise, the saturated brand teal — are present on the page but never composed.

variants.name: Faithful refresh
variants.thesis: All seed dimensions recede. Brand mannerism dominates verbatim. The seven captured weaknesses are corrected under the same IA and section sequence — nothing else moves. The lowest-risk option.
variants.fix: Same IA, same section order. The seven captured improvements applied with extreme care, nothing else moved. The Spring Sale becomes a dismissible promo strip; CHAT demotes to a fixed corner widget; 12 above-fold CTAs collapse to 3 named verbs (Configure · Order Swatches · Find a Showroom); type scale snaps to a 1.2 minor third anchored on the captured 44/36/32/28 ladder; the kit-of-parts surfaces as a 3-piece tile (Seat · Cover · Side) below the hero instead of being buried in carousel slide 1. The brand-team's preferred safe-baseline.

variants.name: Kit-of-parts amplified
variants.thesis: Spatial grammar. Corner-bracket motif at every container, exploded-view hero replaces the carousel, and a configuration grid is the first affordance below the trust band. The captured Sactionals click-together physical assembly becomes the page's visual language.
variants.fix: Spatial grammar. Corner-bracket motifs at every container — sourced verbatim from the literal corner-bracket Sactionals click-together physical assembly. The hero becomes an exploded-view kit composition: Seat · Side · Wedge · Cover laid out side-by-side, dimensioned and labelled. A configuration grid replaces the 8-slide carousel as the first affordance below the trust band. The system isn't shown — the system is the page.

variants.name: Designed For Life
variants.thesis: Temporal grammar. Year-rule hairlines as section delimiters, a chronological heritage filmstrip between hero and product sections, sections sequenced by launch year (Sacs 2006 → Sactionals 2010 → StealthTech 2020). The Designed For Life® registered promise gains visible substrate.
variants.fix: Temporal grammar. Year-rule hairlines (1px + year label) replace standard section margins; a chronological heritage filmstrip sits between hero and product sections; sections are sequenced by product-line launch year — Sacs 2006, Sactionals 2010, StealthTech 2020, Snugg newest. The captured Designed For Life® registered trademark — currently a typographic flourish — finally has an entire IA structure carrying it.

variants.name: v2 · B · Live Build
variants.thesis: The configurator IS the homepage. Live state-driven JS configurator on the canvas, with a Rooms Others Built gallery of captured lifestyle photos as preset configurations. The system isn't shown — it's operated.
variants.fix: The configurator IS the homepage. A live state-driven vanilla-JS configurator occupies the hero canvas — click-to-add Sactional pieces snap to a footprint grid (88px cells = one seat footprint), a bracket-snap micro-animation flashes on placement, swatch chips swap fabric live. A "Rooms Others Built" gallery below loads each captured lifestyle photo as a preset piece-list — click a room, the configurator rebuilds it. Same captured trait as B (modular kit), rotated to the interaction axis.

variants.name: v2 · B · Spec Sheet
variants.thesis: Vitra meets Apple. Inline-SVG line-art exploded-view with dimension callouts on captured photographs, bill-of-materials pricing typography, fabrics treated as specifications. The system is documented.
variants.fix: Vitra meets Apple — technical-drawing aesthetic. Inline-SVG line-art overlays (1px stroke) sit over captured photos with dimension callouts in a system-mono caption face. Pricing renders as a bill-of-materials line-itemed table with a right-aligned mono total; section dividers are dimension-lines, not margins; every fabric becomes a specification. Same captured trait as B (modular kit), rotated to the documentation axis.

current.note: No current.html rendering captured. The original site at https://www.lovesac.com/ is the live before-state.
